title: Rental Application Fee Payment Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rental Application Fee Payment Assistance 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF FAITH HOPE AND CHARITY HOUSING NAVIGATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provide rental deposits and rental application fees. Can provide housing navigation support. 460 SW Madison Avenue Suite 1 Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals with verifiable income Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 740-5807 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE EMERGENCY RENTAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: -Currently have an court dated eviction notice -Imminent risk of homelessness -Household income at or below 65% of Area Medium Income (AMI) Hours: No standard hours (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Familes with children under age 18 who are currently housed and an income level at or below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) with an imminent risk of homelessness. Priority is given to SUN school families who identify as Asian Pacific Islanders or African Immigrants. Hours: No standard hours (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER EMERGENCY RENTAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: -Currently have an court dated eviction notice -Imminent risk of homelessness -Household income at or below 65% of Area Medium Income (AMI) Hours: Not accepting appointments Email (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Familes with children under age 18 who are currently housed and an income level at or below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) with an imminent risk of homelessness. Priority is given to SUN school families who identify as Asian Pacific Islanders or African Immigrants. Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NCH'I WANA HOUSING HOUSING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with application fees, security deposits, move-in costs, and transitional housing in order to help individuals overcome barriers to establishing permanent housing. 2025 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children who identify as Native American and are experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 436-3810 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION HOMEFRONT CRITICAL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides critical financial assistance for documented needs. Types of assistance include: auto payment and repair, child and dependent care, critical baby items, dental, essential home items, food assistance, home repairs, medical, moving and relocation, rent and mortgage, travel and transportation, utilities, and vision care. Home repairs can be considered for all post-9/11 wounded service members. Repairs versus replacements can be considered for deployed eligible service members. Number of times a person can receive assistance: -Active duty member with a rank of E1-E6 may receive 3 total lifetime assists -Currently deployed on Title 10 orders and/are receiving hazardous duty pay may receive 5 total lifetime assists -Documented injury, illness, or wound during post 9/11 service may receive 5 total lifetime assists The number of months of assistance that can be received varies on the type of assistance being requested 17319 San Pedro Avenue Suite 505 San Antonio, TX 78232 Eligibility: Must fit one of three criteria: 1) Wounded, Ill, or Injured Criteria Actively served in the United States military post 9/11 AND suffered a post 9/11 wound, illness, or injury in the line of duty. If discharged, must have Honorable or General (Under Honorable Conditions) discharge within 10 years of discharge. 2) Deployed Criteria A service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS (Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System) eligible/legal dependents AND one of the following criteria's met: -Currently deployed to an overseas location and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -Assigned to a ship that has been at sea for 30+ days and is en route to theater, forward deployed, or otherwise on a mission status, not a training status -On unaccompanied, overseas PCS orders and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -A National Guard member or Reservist on Title 10 orders (not Title 32) who either: Will deploy to an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater in the next 90 days or has returned from an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater within the past 180 days 3) General Criteria -An active duty service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS eligible/legal dependents Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week, leave a message and a coordinator will call back Intake Coordinators: Monday-Friday 7am-3pm Email (210) 659-7756 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES OREGON EVICTION PREVENTION RAPID RESPONSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with past-due rent and future rent up to one month. Provides rental fees and rental security deposits, which may include pet fees, housing-related legal fees, partial payments to reduce the housing debt, and application fees. Utility assistance, such as electricity, gas, water, and sewage (paid to the landlord or utility company), and repairs and damage costs up to $2,500. Assistance is not guaranteed and is based on funding availability. A household can receive assistance only once during the Oregon Housing and Community Services (OHCS) biennium cycle (July 2025-June 2027). Eligibility: -Household must have an active eviction court case number and court date -Household income is at or below 50% of the Area Median Income (AMI) Hours: Applications open on the 1st business day of the month 9am-until capacity is reached Email (833) 746-8167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAITH HOPE AND CHARITY HOUSING NAVIGATION IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION EMERGENCY RENTAL ASSISTANCE IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION EMERGENCY RENTAL ASSISTANCE NCH'I WANA HOUSING HOUSING ASSISTANCE
